solution_summary: Fix the flight seat availability arithmetic in the backend seat inventory counter so available seats are computed as total_seats - booked_seats instead of total_seats + booked_seats. Keep the change localized to the seat inventory DB helper unless validation shows a shared downstream helper also hardcodes the same formula.
implementation_steps: Inspect backend/app/db/seat_inventory.py, especially seat_inventory_counts, to confirm where available seat counts are calculated and whether the logic is a direct arithmetic expression or part of an aggregate query/alias.
Update the availability computation to subtract booked seats from total seats, preserving existing returned fields and query shape.

Check for any shared helper or duplicated computation used by flight details; if the same formula exists elsewhere in the seat inventory path, align it to the corrected arithmetic while avoiding unrelated refactors.
Review any tests or fixtures that assert available seat counts and adjust expectations to match the corrected calculation.
Add or update a focused regression test that verifies available seats never exceed total aircraft capacity for a representative flight scenario.

planned_changes.change_summary: Correct the available-seat calculation in seat_inventory_counts from total + booked to total - booked.
planned_changes.rationale: This is the issue-identified source of the bug and should be the primary, minimal fix to align flight details with expected seat availability.

planned_changes.change_summary: Add or update a regression test covering flight details seat availability to assert available seats equal total minus booked.
planned_changes.rationale: A targeted test will prevent reintroduction of the arithmetic bug and validate the backend path used by flight details.

patch_strategy: Use a minimal, localized patch. First, adjust the arithmetic in seat_inventory_counts so the computed available seats represent remaining capacity. Keep the existing API/data contract unchanged. Then validate whether flight details consume this value directly; if so, ensure no further transformation re-inflates the count. Add a regression test at the service level or nearest existing test layer that exercises a flight with booked seats and asserts available_seats == total_seats - booked_seats, including a check that the result never exceeds aircraft capacity. Avoid broader schema/model changes unless the test run reveals a duplicated formula in another shared helper.
validation_steps: Run the targeted test suite covering flight details and seat inventory counts.
Add a regression case with known total and booked seat counts, verifying available seats are computed by subtraction.
Verify the returned API payload shape is unchanged and only the numeric value differs.
Confirm no flight detail response reports available seats greater than total aircraft capacity in the tested scenario.
If available, run a broader backend test pass around seat inventory and flight service paths to ensure no shared helper still applies the incorrect formula.
pr_title: SDLC-67: Fix inflated available seat count on flight details
pr_body:
## Summary
Fix the backend seat availability calculation so flight details report available seats as `total_seats - booked_seats` instead of the inflated `total_seats + booked_seats` result.

## Changes
- Correct the availability arithmetic in `backend/app/db/seat_inventory.py` (`seat_inventory_counts`)
- Add/adjust regression coverage for flight details seat availability

## Validation
- Targeted tests for seat inventory and flight details
- Regression assertion that available seats never exceed aircraft capacity

## Notes
- Change is intentionally localized and preserves the existing API/data model shape.
risks: There may be a duplicated availability formula in a downstream service/helper if flight details apply additional transformation.
Existing tests or seed data may implicitly assert the incorrect inflated value and need updates.
If booked seats can exceed total seats due to data inconsistencies, subtraction may surface negative availability and may require clamping or data cleanup policy review.
assumptions: seat_inventory_counts is the authoritative backend source for flight details available-seat reporting.
No API contract change is needed; only the computed value should change.
The incorrect formula is localized and not intentionally used elsewhere for a different metric.
